flashcards.definition: A point where a common factor cancels; the function is undefined but the limit exists.
A vertical asymptote where the function → ±∞; the limit does not exist finitely.
Occurs when left-hand and right-hand limits exist but are not equal.
A factor present in both numerator and denominator that, when canceled, creates a hole.
At x = c: substitute c into the simplified expression to find the y-value.
